articles.headline: Fudd, Mabrey, Howard among WNBA All-Star 3-point contestants
articles.description: Azzi Fudd will participate in the 3-point contest as part of WNBA All-Star Weekend in Chicago, joined by Rhyne Howard, Marina Mabrey, Bridget Carleton, Natisha Hiedeman and Janelle Salaun.

articles.headline: Love her or hate her, Sophie Cunningham won't be ignored
articles.description: Without a starting role and without apology, the Fever guard has become a star and a dividing line.

images.name: Sophie Cunningham lede [1296x729]
images.caption: Sophie Cunningham, now in her eighth WNBA season, has had a meteoric rise in recognition over the past year.
images.alt: WNBA player Sophie Cunningham photographed at Mizzou Rec Center in Columbia, MO, on April 10, 2026.

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 02 [2086x1391]
images.caption: Immediately following her infamous tussle with Jacy Sheldon, Google searches for "Sophie Cunningham" spiked 800%.

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 02 [3500x2333]
images.caption: "I am not the enforcer," Cunningham insists. "I am not a bodyguard. I am just going to do whatever the team needs."

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 03 [3500x2529]
images.caption: The Cunningham lore started early in central Missouri, where Paula and Jim had to carry a copy of Sophie's birth certificate for doubters, and everyone knew if you messed with Lindsey (bottom right, with Sophie at Mizzou) you'd be hearing from her sister.
images.credit: Courtesy of the Cunningham's; Eric Gay/AP

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 04 [3498x4676]
images.caption: "Playing against her, it's annoying as crap," says Cunningham's former teammate Brittney Griner, above. "But when she's your teammate, you wouldn't want anybody else."
images.credit: Matt York/AP; Ron Hoskins/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 04 [864x1159]
images.caption: In high school, above, and college, Cunningham left her mark: "The way that we were able to jell and play together and trust each other and feel safe with each other? Sophie led the way in that," Tigers teammate Hannah Schuchts says.

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 06 [6823x4585]
images.caption: "I think it was so dumb," Cunningham said on her podcast about her viral point. "But it was pissing her off, and I couldn't help myself. I could not."
images.credit: Grace Hollars/IndyStar / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 01 [3500x2333]
images.caption: After rehabbing a torn MCL that ended her 2025 season, Cunningham has returned to average 23.0 minutes and 9.4 points per game.

images.name: Sophie Cunningham 05 [3500x2333]
images.caption: "Everyone wants to look the same. Everyone wants the same vibe," Cunningham says. "Screw that. I'm gonna be me."

images.name: The best of Sophie Cunningham's season
images.caption: The best of Sophie Cunningham's season

articles.headline: Kayla McBride and Olivia Miles lead Lynx to 6th straight win, 105-102 over Storm
articles.description: — Kayla McBride made six 3-pointers and scored 33 points, rookie Olivia Miles had 32 points, eight rebounds and eight assists, and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Seattle Storm 105-102 on Monday night for their sixth straight victory.

articles.headline: Kiki Iriafen scores 23 points as Mystics snap Valkyries' 9-game winning streak with 90-82 victory
articles.description: — Kiki Iriafen had 23 points and 12 rebounds, Shakira Austin finished with 15 points and 15 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics rallied past Golden State for a 90-82 victory Monday night to snap the Valkyries' nine-game winning streak.

article.headline: Azzi Fudd scores 22 in 1st WNBA start and helps the Wings beat the Aces 95-87
article.description: — Azzi Fudd scored 22 points in her first WNBA start, Jessica Shepard had her second triple-double of the season and the Dallas Wings beat the Las Vegas Aces 95-87 on Thursday night.
article.linkText: Azzi Fudd scores 22 in 1st WNBA start and helps the Wings beat the Aces 95-87

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Dallas Wings vs. Las Vegas Aces - Game Highlights) 2026/05/29 ESHEET

article.story: ARLINGTON, Texas -- — Azzi Fudd scored 22 points in her first WNBA start, Jessica Shepard had her second triple-double of the season and the Dallas Wings beat the Las Vegas Aces 95-87 on Thursday night. Shepard also scored 22 points to go with a career-high 20 rebounds and 10 assists. Her other triple-double this season (18 points, 10 rebounds and 12 assists) came in a 99-89 win over Chicago on May 20. Shepard scored 11 points in the third quarter, including a shot in the lane to tie it at 72 in the closing seconds. Shepard also opened the fourth with a basket to give Dallas its first lead, 74-72, since it led 17-16. Shepard secured a triple-double when she found Awak Kuier for a 3-pointer with 2:57 remaining in the fourth to make it 90-81. Then, Paige Bueckers added a 3-pointer from the top of the key for a 12-point lead. Bueckers added 20 points and six assists for Dallas (5-3), which lost five straight games to the Aces last season. Kuier finished with 12 points and Arike Ogunbowale was limited to just 18 minutes due to an illness. Fudd, the No. 1 pick in the draft in April, was coming off a 24-point performance as a reserve against New York. A’ja Wilson scored 21 points for Las Vegas (4-3), which has lost back-to-back games. Jackie Young added 15 points and Chennedy Carter scored 12 of her 14 points in the first half. NaLyssa Smith grabbed 12 rebounds to go with nine points. Las Vegas was outscored 50-34 in the second half. Up next Las Vegas continues a three-game trip at Golden State on Sunday. Dallas hosts Seattle on Monday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
